subject: Public Health
title: Tuberculosis: The White Plague
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Tuberculosis: The White Plague.

hook.text: TB killed one in four Europeans during the 19th century—earning the name 'the white plague.'

buildup.text: It was romanticized in art and literature—pale, thin poets wasting away were considered beautiful.

discovery.text: Robert Koch identified the tuberculosis bacterium in 1882, proving it was infectious, not inherited.

twist.text: TB never went away. It still kills 1.3 million people annually—more than HIV or malaria.

climax.text: Drug-resistant strains are spreading, making treatment longer, harder, and far more expensive.

punchline.text: The 'romantic' disease is the world's deadliest infection.

quiz.question: How many people does TB still kill each year?
